channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Six People Missing After Boat Capsizes in Yamuna River, Rescue Efforts Underway in Hamirpur
item.description: On Wednesday evening, a tragic incident occurred in Kutubpur Patiya hamlet under Bhauli gram panchayat in Hamirpur, Uttar Pradesh, when a boat carrying nine people, excluding the boatman, capsized in ...

item.title: Federal High Court Convicts Former Power Minister Saleh Mamman Over ₦33.8bn Fraud Case
item.description: Federal High Court for Abuja don find former Minister of Power, Saleh Mamman, guilty on 12 counts wey relate to fraud and money laundering, after EFCC prosecute am. Justice James Omotosho talk say pro...

item.title: Evaluating the Impact and Effectiveness of South Africa’s Commissions of Inquiry
item.description: Questions around the cost and impact of South Africa’s commissions of inquiry have intensified, particularly as the Madlanga Commission faces a potential six-month extension amidst funding challenges....

item.title: Five Podcasts That Encourage Learning and Connection Between Parents and Kids
item.description: As families balance screen time with reading, podcasts are emerging as a convenient and enriching middle ground. They fit naturally into everyday routines, such as car trips, cooking, or bedtime, wher...

item.title: Man arrested on suspicion of handling stolen phone linked to Morgan McSweeney case
item.description: A 28-year-old man has been arrested by the Metropolitan Police on suspicion of handling stolen goods in connection with the theft of a mobile phone belonging to Morgan McSweeney, the former chief of s...

item.title: Comelec Confirms Preparations Underway for 2028 Philippine Elections
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) has clarified any doubts regarding the 2028 national elections, affirming that preparations are already in progress. Comelec Chairperson George Erwin Garcia respo...

item.title: Authorities Trace Passengers After Hantavirus Cases on Cruise Ship
item.description: Several countries have started monitoring and tracking passengers from the MV Hondius cruise ship after an outbreak of hantavirus was reported. The virus claimed three lives — a Dutch couple and a Ger...

item.title: Fani-Kayode Redeployed to South Africa After Personal Request, Denies Any Rejection by Germany
item.description: Femi Fani-Kayode don confirm say President Bola Ahmed Tinubu approve him redeployment as Nigeria ambassador-designate to South Africa after him personally request to shift from Germany. Originally, Fa...

item.title: Brian Michael Hinds, Known as Bottom G, Builds Identity Beyond Andrew Tate Comparison
item.description: Brian Michael Hinds, also known as Bottom G, gained viral fame due to his uncanny resemblance to controversial influencer Andrew Tate, though their personalities are polar opposites. While Tate is kno...

item.title: How to Avoid Spreading Misinformation on Social Media
item.description: Social media has become a primary source of news for over half of Australian adults, yet the majority of content shared is not properly fact-checked. Many influencers and content creators, often unqua...

item.title: Minister Ntshavheni Provides Update on Recent Cabinet Decisions
item.description: Minister in the Presidency, Khumbudzo Ntshavheni, held a media briefing on Thursday to outline the key outcomes of the latest cabinet meeting. During the session, she provided insights into government...

item.title: Malawi Parliament Launches Third Inquiry into 2024 Military Plane Crash Killing Vice-President Chilima
item.description: Malawi’s parliament has initiated its third formal investigation into the June 2024 military aircraft crash that claimed the life of former vice-president Saulos Chilima and eight others. The parliame...

item.title: PSG and Arsenal Prepare for 2026 UEFA Champions League Final in Budapest
item.description: The stage is set for a thrilling European football night as Paris Saint-Germain (PSG) and Arsenal FC get ready for the 2026 UEFA Champions League final at Puskás Aréna, Budapest on May 30. Kick-off is...

item.title: DAAR Communications Flags Discrepancies in Shareholding Records Despite CAC Response
item.description: DAAR Communications Plc don come out clear say any wahala wey dey show for their shareholding records for Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) portal no match wetin dey their official books. Dis clarifi...

item.title: Government announces $10.7 billion boost to national fuel reserves
item.description: The federal government has unveiled a substantial $10.7 billion plan to strengthen Australia's fuel reserves, aiming to shield the nation from potential supply shocks. The announcement comes just a mo...

item.title: Bihar BPSC 72nd CCE 2026 Recruitment: Revised Applications Open
item.description: The Bihar Public Service Commission (BPSC) has announced the commencement of the revised application process for the 72nd Combined Competitive Examination (CCE) for the year 2026. Candidates intereste...

item.title: Registrar halts Jubilee leadership changes after objection, affecting Matiang’i’s deputy leader position
item.description: Former Interior Cabinet Secretary Fred Matiang’i has hit a stumbling block in his attempt to formally take up a senior role within the Jubilee Party after the Registrar of Political Parties declined t...

item.title: ABS Survey Reveals Declines in Trust, Health and Financial Wellbeing Among Australians
item.description: The latest Australian Bureau of Statistics General Social Survey, conducted in May and June 2025, highlights notable declines in how Australians perceive their quality of life. Trust in people and ins...

item.title: Australian authorities receive returning ISIS-affiliated women and their children amid legal proceedings
item.description: A group of Australian women linked to ISIS, along with their children, have returned from Syria, landing at Melbourne and Sydney international airports. The women, including grandmother Kawsar Abbas, ...

item.title: Tuareg Separatists and Jihadists Capture Dozens of Malian Soldiers in Northern Offensive
item.description: Na so di situation dey for Mali as Tuareg separatist groups and jihadist fighters don capture plenty Malian soldiers after dem launch coordinated attacks on military positions across di country. Di ma...
